versions.label: BSB
versions.text: I always thank my God, remembering you in my prayers, because I hear about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints. I pray that your partnership in the faith may become effective as you fully acknowledge every good thing that is ours in Christ. I take great joy and encouragement in your love, because you, brother, have refreshed the hearts of the saints.

context_lines: This section establishes the moral ground for everything Paul will ask next. He is not flattering Philemon mechanically; he is calling attention to the Christian character Philemon already claims and displays.
common_interpretation_lines: Paul begins with thanksgiving and prayer, but the point is not merely politeness. He highlights Philemon's **faith**, **love**, and history of **refreshing the hearts of the saints** so that the coming appeal will rest on Philemon's lived identity in Christ.

In other words, Paul is not preparing Philemon for a legal argument. He is preparing him for a Christian one. What follows is going to test whether Philemon's love reaches as far as his confession says it should.
current_position_lines: This site's current position is that **Philemon 1:4-7** shows prayer functioning as thanksgiving, spiritual concern, and joyful recognition of grace already at work in another believer. It is not a request system aimed at securing private outcomes.
Paul's prayer is relational and kingdom-oriented. He gives thanks, asks for growth in shared faith, and ties the whole thing to the refreshment of the saints.
